title: Accuracy Metrics
text: Click Accuracy button (bar chart icon) header. modal shows metrics calculated across verified documents connected database. Metrics meaningful verified documents (reviewed_at timestamp). Unverified documents unknown ground truth excluded.

title: Accuracy Metrics
text: accuracy calculation compares LLM’s original output human-corrected ground truth verified document. Three things can happen record review:

title: Accuracy Metrics
text: Recall – fraction real records LLM find? Recall=LLM records kept editedLLM records kept edited+records added reviewer\text{Recall} = \frac{\text{LLM records kept edited}}{\text{LLM records kept edited} + \text{records added reviewer}} low recall means LLM missing records present papers. Precision – fraction LLM records real? Precision=LLM records kept editedLLM records kept edited+records deleted reviewer\text{Precision} = \frac{\text{LLM records kept edited}}{\text{LLM records kept edited} + \text{records deleted reviewer}} low precision means LLM hallucinating records don’t exist. F1 – harmonic mean precision recall. single summary detection performance.

title: Accuracy Metrics
text: Record detection tells half story – found record may still incorrect field values. Field-level metrics measure accurately individual fields filled . Field precision – across non-deleted, LLM-extracted records, fraction field values correct (unedited)? Major edit rate – fraction records required least one edit “major” field (fields uniquely identify record schema, required fields)? Average edits per document – many field corrections needed per verified document average.

title: Accuracy Metrics
text: ranges rough guides – right threshold depends schema complexity domain.

title: Accuracy Metrics
text: Accuracy metrics also available via ecoextract::calculate_accuracy(): detailed explanation metric derived, see ACCURACY.md ecoextract repository.
code: accuracy <- ecoextract::calculate_accuracy("ecoextract_records.db")  # Detection metrics accuracy$detection_recall accuracy$detection_precision accuracy$detection_f1  # Field-level metrics accuracy$field_precision accuracy$major_edit_rate accuracy$avg_edits_per_document  # Per-document breakdown accuracy$by_document

title: Accuracy Metrics
text: Accuracy metrics guide prompt schema iteration: Low recall → LLM missing records. Review extraction prompt – add examples, clarify counts record, check papers many records lower recall . Low precision → LLM hallucinating. Tighten schema’s required fields add negative examples prompt (“extract X Y absent”). Low field precision specific columns → fields ambiguous prompt schema. Add definition, examples, edge-case guidance fields. High major edit rate → unique/required fields frequently wrong. impactful errors – prioritise fixing prompt. typical iteration loop: Process small batch (10-20 papers) Review ecoreview, click Verify Check Accuracy modal Update ecoextract/extraction_prompt.md ecoextract/schema.json Force re-extract batch compare metrics satisfied, process full corpus

title: Accuracy Metrics
text: Use Export Records CSV get flat file suitable analysis R Excel. export includes: non-deleted records joined document metadata record_id tracing back database reviewed_at row (document join) filter verified records
code: library(readr) library(dplyr)  records <- read_csv("myproject_records_20260706.csv")  # Verified documents only verified <- records |> filter(!is.na(reviewed_at))  # Quick summary cat("Documents verified:", n_distinct(verified$document_id), "\n") cat("Total records:", nrow(verified), "\n")

text: row one extracted record. Columns defined ecoextract schema. table supports: Inline editing – double-click cell edit Per-column search – filter boxes column header Multi-row selection – click, Shift+click, Ctrl/Cmd+click Column management – click Columns reorder show/hide columns via drag--drop

title: EcoReview: Getting Started
text: left side split view shows OCR text current document. click record row, supporting sentences highlighted OCR text. Hover highlighted sentence tooltip listing evidence sentences record. Alternatively, click PDF switch original PDF viewer (requires PDF file accessible).

title: EcoReview: Getting Started
text: typical per-document workflow: Select document dropdown Read OCR text left; review extracted records right Edit incorrect fields – double-click cells correct values Add missing records – click Add Row, fill new row Delete hallucinated records – select row(s), click Delete Click Verify – marks document reviewed (reviewed_at timestamp set) Verified documents show ✓ dropdown. badge sidebar counts X Y unreviewed.

title: EcoReview: Getting Started
text: Downloads non-deleted records joined document metadata. Wide columns (OCR text, reasoning) excluded readability. Sorted document_id record_id.

title: EcoReview: Getting Started
text: Downloads pipeline status summary documents: document_id, file_name, first_author_lastname, publication_year, title, ocr_status, metadata_status, extraction_status, refinement_status.

title: EcoReview: Getting Started
text: Downloads current .db file edits full audit trail intact.

title: EcoReview: Getting Started
text: Split large database across multiple reviewers, merge done: combine_db() validates parts share schema merging warns overlapping document IDs.
code: # Split into 4 parts -- part 1 gets the lowest document IDs (sequential default) parts <- ecoreview::split_db("ecoextract_records.db", n = 4) # → ecoextract_records_part_1.db ... ecoextract_records_part_4.db  # Random assignment instead parts <- ecoreview::split_db("ecoextract_records.db", n = 4,                               random = TRUE, seed = 42)  # Each reviewer opens their part run_app(db_path = "ecoextract_records_part_2.db")  # Recombine when all reviewers are done ecoreview::combine_db(   "path/to/parts/",   output_path = "ecoextract_records_reviewed.db" )

title: EcoReview: Getting Started
text: Reviewing Extraction Output – editing, adding, deleting records depth Accuracy Metrics – interpreting accuracy modal ?run_app, ?split_db, ?combine_db – function reference

title: Reviewing Extraction Output
text: Select document dropdown left sidebar. table loads document’s extracted records; OCR viewer loads text. Filtering list: Show unreviewed (default ) hides documents already reviewed_at timestamp Show document IDs switches labels numeric IDs sorted ascending – useful jumping known ID badge shows X Y unreviewed based unique, non-NULL document IDs.

title: Reviewing Extraction Output
text: OCR panel shows markdown text extracted paper ohseer/Mistral. renders tables, headings, inline formatting. Use cross-check extracted records original source. Evidence highlighting: click row records table, sentences all_supporting_source_sentences highlighted orange OCR panel. Hovering highlighted sentence shows tooltip listing evidence sentences row, ⚠️ warning sentence located OCR text. PDF view: click PDF tab switch embedded PDF viewer. PDF path resolved database; found, viewer shows “PDF Available.” Use run_app(pdf_dir = "path//pdfs") point app folder PDFs stored separately database.

title: Reviewing Extraction Output
text: Double-click cell edit inline. cell becomes input; press Enter click elsewhere confirm. Changes applied values$extracted_df immediately saved database click Verify. Audit trail: every cell edit recorded record_edits table original value, new value, column name, timestamp. powers accuracy metrics. Field types: cells free-text. schema-defined types enforced extraction review – can type value.

Deleted rows soft-deleted – deleted_by_user timestamp set rather row removed. remain database appear greyed Show deleted checked. means: audit trail preserved Accuracy metrics correctly identify records reviewer removed can restore deleted record selecting clicking Restore

title: Reviewing Extraction Output
text: Click Verify (Accept) mark current document reviewed. : Saves pending edits database via ecoextract::save_document() Sets reviewed_at document row Moves next unreviewed document Show unreviewed document name gets ✓ prefix dropdown. Double-clicking Verify safe – app reloads current database state immediately save, second click compares already-saved rows produces duplicates.

Click Show make everything visible , Hide collapse nothing. column order Visible list determines left--right order table. Column changes display-– underlying database never modified. Priority columns: set priority_cols run_app() pin important columns left default: Visible columns: set visible_cols show subset load:
code: run_app(   db_path = "records.db",   priority_cols = c("Pathogen_Name", "Host_Name", "Detection_Result_Direction") ) run_app(   db_path = "records.db",   visible_cols = c("Pathogen_Name", "Host_Name", "Observation_Type") )

title: Reviewing Extraction Output
text: connect database, ecoreview checks records.id column type. database uses older (pre-UUID) integer schema, warning modal appears: Blocking modal (id INTEGER, primary key): schema assign IDs automatically. add edit records run ecoextract::migrate_ecoextract_database("path//db"). Advisory modal (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY): saving works, databases merged combine_db() migrated. Dismiss continue, migrate first.

title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
text: Use priority_cols pin important columns left, visible_cols show columns reviewers need. Neither parameter affects underlying database — display-settings. Reviewers can adjust column order visibility time using Columns button table, opens drag--drop panel Visible Hidden zones.
code: run_app(   title = "ChiroScan: Bat Interaction Review",   # Show these columns first (left-most), in this order   priority_cols = c("Pathogen_Name", "Host_Name",                     "Detection_Result_Direction", "Observation_Type"),   # Show only these columns on load (others hidden but still editable via Columns button)   visible_cols  = c("Pathogen_Name", "Host_Name",                     "Detection_Result_Direction", "Observation_Type",                     "Host_Species", "Pathogen_Species") )

title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
text: Side--side document review: View OCR text original PDF alongside extracted records Inline cell editing: Edit extracted data directly table change tracking Column management: Reorder show/hide columns via drag--drop panel — database never modified Evidence highlighting: Click record highlight supporting text document Audit trail: Full history edits made review Accuracy metrics: Precision, recall, F1 scores calculated verified documents Document navigation: Toggle dropdown filename document ID display jump directly known ID Export Records CSV: Downloads non-deleted records joined document metadata, wide columns (OCR text, reasoning) stripped readability Export Documents CSV: Downloads summary documents — ID, filename, author, year, title, pipeline statuses (OCR, metadata, extraction, refinement) Save Database: Download current SQLite file edits applied Schema migration warning: Alerts connect database uses pre-UUID schema requires migration

title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
text: R >= 4.0 ecoextract package (database output)

what: Getting Started with the Full Workflow
title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
text: ’re new ecosystem, start ecoextract: See ecoextract documentation complete workflow details, including API setup, custom schemas, parallel processing.
code: # 1. Install the extraction packages pak::pak("n8layman/ohseer") pak::pak("n8layman/ecoextract")  # 2. Process your PDFs library(ecoextract) results <- process_documents(   pdf_path = "path/to/pdfs/",   db_conn = "ecoextract_records.db" )  # 3. Review and validate the results renv::install("n8layman/ecoreview") library(ecoreview) run_app(db_path = "ecoextract_records.db")

title: Interactive Review Interface for Ecological Data Extraction
text: ecoreview::split_db() divides database n roughly-equal part files multiple reviewers can work subset independently. ecoreview::combine_db() merges finished parts back single database, accepting either directory path (auto-discovers *_part_N.db files) explicit vector paths. writing data, combine_db() validates parts share schema errors clear message differ, preventing silent data corruption mismatched versions. Opening combined database run_app() gives full accuracy metrics across reviewers — verified documents, field-level edits, deletion flags preserved exactly, accuracy modal reflects complete picture review.
code: # Split into 4 sequential parts (lowest document IDs first) — written next to the source file parts <- ecoreview::split_db("ecoextract_records.db", n = 4) # → ecoextract_records_part_1.db ... ecoextract_records_part_4.db  # Random assignment instead of sequential parts <- ecoreview::split_db("ecoextract_records.db", n = 4, random = TRUE, seed = 42)  # Each reviewer launches the app pointed at their own part ecoreview::run_app(db_path = "ecoextract_records_part_2.db")  # Once all reviewers are done, recombine (supply directory or explicit paths) ecoreview::combine_db(   "path/to/parts/",   output_path = "ecoextract_records_reviewed.db" )

what: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
title: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
text: Merges multiple part databases (created split_db()) one, verifying parts share identical schema proceeding.

title: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
code: combine_db(   db_paths,   output_path,   overwrite = FALSE,   pattern = "_part_\\d+\\.db$" )

title: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
text: db_paths character vector >= 2 part database file paths, single directory path. directory supplied, pattern used discover part files within . output_path Path combined output database. Must already exist unless overwrite = TRUE. overwrite TRUE, delete output_path combining. Default FALSE. pattern Regular expression used filter files db_paths directory. Defaults "_part_\\d+\\.db$", matches naming convention produced split_db().

title: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
text: Path combined database (invisibly).

title: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
text: db_paths can supplied two ways: character vector file paths (existing behaviour). single directory path — files directory whose names match pattern collected automatically sorted merging. Schema check: table names, column names, column types must match across parts. Mismatched tables columns raise error; mismatched column types raise warning (SQLite's flexible typing often makes harmless, investigate). Overlap check: document IDs expected disjoint across parts. overlap reported warning; overlapping rows still inserted (duplicate skipped INSERT IGNORE). Primary-key remapping: tables carry auto-increment integer primary key reference interaction_id (e.g. human_edits) inserted without original PK SQLite assigns new non-conflicting values. foreign-key relationships preserved interaction IDs unique across parts.

title: Combine part databases back into a single database — combine_db
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Supply a directory — part files discovered automatically combine_db("review_parts/", output_path = "extractions_reviewed.db")  # Supply paths explicitly parts <- list.files("review_parts", pattern = "_part_\\d+\\.db$",                     full.names = TRUE) combine_db(parts, output_path = "extractions_reviewed.db") } # }

what: Run the ecoreview Shiny application — run_app
title: Run the ecoreview Shiny application — run_app
text: Launches interactive Shiny interface reviewing validating ecological data extracted ecoextract.

title: Run the ecoreview Shiny application — run_app
code: run_app(   title = "EcoReview: Data Validation",   app_name = "EcoReview",   github_url = "https://github.com/n8layman/ecoreview",   export_prefix = "ecoextract",   db_path = NULL,   priority_cols = NULL,   visible_cols = NULL,   pdf_dir = NULL,   ... )

title: Run the ecoreview Shiny application — run_app
text: title Application title displayed browser tab header app_name Short name application (used export filenames) github_url Optional GitHub repository URL source link. Set NULL hide. export_prefix Prefix exported CSV filenames (default: "ecoextract") db_path Optional path SQLite database file connect startup. supplied file exists, app opens immediately without showing connect modal. NULL (default) shows connect modal. priority_cols Character vector column names display first (left-). Columns present data silently ignored. columns follow original order. NULL (default) uses data frame column order. visible_cols Character vector column names show. columns hidden table display (underlying data unchanged). NULL (default) shows columns. pdf_dir Optional path directory containing PDF files. Use .db file stored separately PDFs app launched different working directory. path stored database resolve, app falls back looking file's basename . NULL (default) uses path stored database. ... Additional arguments passed shiny::runApp()

title: Run the ecoreview Shiny application — run_app
text: Launches Shiny application (return)

title: Run the ecoreview Shiny application — run_app
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Run with defaults run_app()  # Customize for a specific project with priority and visible columns run_app(   title = "ChiroScan: Bat Interaction Review",   app_name = "ChiroScan",   github_url = "https://github.com/n8layman/bat-interactions",   export_prefix = "bat_interactions",   priority_cols = c("Pathogen_Name", "Host_Name",                     "Detection_Result_Direction", "Observation_Type"),   visible_cols  = c("Pathogen_Name", "Host_Name",                     "Detection_Result_Direction", "Observation_Type",                     "Host_Species", "Pathogen_Species") ) } # }

what: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
title: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
text: Distributes documents evenly across n part databases multiple reviewers can work independently. part receives full schema plus subset documents (associated records audit trail entries) assigned part.

title: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
code: split_db(   db_path,   n,   output_dir = NULL,   prefix = NULL,   random = FALSE,   seed = NULL )

title: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
text: db_path Path source SQLite database. n Integer >= 2. Number parts create. output_dir Directory output part files. Defaults directory db_path. prefix Filename prefix part files. Defaults source filename (without extension). Parts named {prefix}_part_01.db, {prefix}_part_02.db, etc. random Logical. FALSE (default), documents assigned ascending document_id order part gets consecutive block IDs. TRUE, documents shuffled randomly assignment. seed Optional integer seed reproducible random assignment random = TRUE. Ignored random = FALSE.

title: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
text: Character vector paths created part databases (invisibly).

title: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
text: reviewers finish, recombine combine_db().

title: Split a database into roughly equal pieces for parallel review — split_db
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Sequential split (default): part 1 gets lowest IDs, part 2 next block, etc. parts <- split_db("extractions.db", n = 4)  # Random split with a reproducible seed parts <- split_db("extractions.db", n = 4, random = TRUE, seed = 42,                   output_dir = "review_parts") } # }

title: ecoreview 0.1.41
text: run_app(db_path = ...) parameter: pass path SQLite database app connects automatically startup, skipping connect modal. Man pages regenerated: split_db.Rd now documents random updated seed parameters. run_app.Rd documents db_path.

title: ecoreview 0.1.40
text: Document count badge uses unique non-NULL document IDs: total unreviewed counts now count distinct document_id values, excluding NULLs. Previously nrow() used, NULL-id rows (malformed databases) inflated counts.

title: ecoreview 0.1.39
text: Crash startup certain databases: “missing value TRUE/FALSE needed” document-selector observer need_force_load evaluated NA (triggered document’s document_id NA, making == comparison return NA). Guarded isTRUE().

title: ecoreview 0.1.38
text: Curated CSV exports: records export drops wide/unreadable columns (all_supporting_source_sentences, extraction_reasoning, refinement_reasoning). Documents export now fixed narrow view: document_id, file_name, first_author_lastname, publication_year, title, pipeline statuses (ocr_status, metadata_status, extraction_status, refinement_status). Missing columns silently skipped via dplyr::any_of().

title: ecoreview 0.1.36
text: Separate CSV export buttons: single “Export CSV” button now two buttons – “Export Records CSV” (records joined document metadata, sorted document_id/record_id) “Export Documents CSV” (documents table via ecoextract::get_documents()).

title: ecoreview 0.1.35
text: split_db explicit random flag: split_db() gains random parameter (default FALSE). FALSE, documents split ascending document_id order (sequential blocks). TRUE, documents shuffled splitting; combine seed reproducibility. Previously mode inferred whether seed set.

title: ecoreview 0.1.33
text: Document ID mode sorts numerically: “Show document IDs” checked, dropdown sorts document_id (numeric) instead filename, IDs appear order.

title: ecoreview 0.1.32
text: Toggle document IDs dropdown: “Show document IDs” checkbox document selector switches dropdown labels filenames raw document_id integers, making easy navigate directly known ID.

title: ecoreview 0.1.31
text: CSV export groups rows document: records exported insertion order, manually-added rows (inserted initial extraction) appeared end file rather alongside records paper. Export now sorts document_id record_id.

title: ecoreview 0.1.30
text: Package versions title bar: header now shows ecoreview vX.Y.Z | ecoextract vX.Y.Z users can report exact versions without digging R session info.

title: ecoreview 0.1.29
text: pk = 0 (plain integer, auto-assign): blocking modal — saves silently duplicate records every verify. User must run ecoextract::migrate_ecoextract_database(path) continuing. pk = 1 (auto-assign works, UUID): dismissible advisory modal — saving safe databases merged migrated.

title: ecoreview 0.1.27
text: UUID-ready id handling: get_all_edited_cells() get_restored_interaction_ids() now initialise character(0) instead integer(0), split_combine.R accumulates interaction ids character vector. Prepares ecoreview ecoextract’s UUID v4 record ids without type-coercion issues.

title: ecoreview 0.1.26
text: Narrow id column DataTable: id column capped 60 px text-overflow ellipsis dominate view. columns keep existing 200 px cap. Hover tooltip still shows full value.

title: ecoreview 0.1.25
text: Row duplication double-clicking Verify (Smithsonian/OHP issue #4): first verify, values$original_df never updated initial 0-row state. subsequent verify call saw user-added row (id = NA) “added” re-inserted , creating duplicate records. Fixed reloading original_df fresh database immediately save_document() call, diff always compares true current DB state. Regression test added inst/test_add_record_no_existing.R (test 5b).

title: ecoreview 0.1.24
text: resolve_pdf_path exported: function marked @keywords internal called via ecoreview:: app, causing error installed builds. Exported properly; find_project_root exported well consistency.

title: ecoreview 0.1.23
text: “missing value TRUE/FALSE needed” adding records zero-record documents (Smithsonian/OHP issue #4): record-ID renaming loop (triggered publication year first author changed) compared old_id != new_record_id without guarding NA. User-added rows record_id = NA first save; NA != x returns NA, (NA) throws. Fixed !.na(old_id) && guard. Regression test added inst/test_add_record_no_existing.R.

title: ecoreview 0.1.22
text: Explicit pdf_dir override (highest priority — set connect modal via run_app(pdf_dir = ...)) Stored path -Stored path relative .db file’s directory Stored path relative project root (found walking db directory .git, .Rproj, DESCRIPTION, .marker found) Stored path relative working directory run_app() called PDF Folder field connect modal: Browse button lets users set PDF directory interactively without restarting app.

title: ecoreview 0.1.21
text: pdf_dir parameter run_app() (issue #26): initial implementation — basename fallback .

title: ecoreview 0.1.20
text: Per-column row filtering (issue #15): enabled DataTables filter = 'top' column header gets search input (text box free-text columns, select dropdown low-cardinality ones). Filtering entirely client-side (server = FALSE) affect data indices, row selection, OCR highlighting, sentence match lookups remain correct.

title: ecoreview 0.1.19
text: “Show ” button columns modal (issue #18): added “Show ” button alongside existing “Hide ” button. Clicking moves every column Hidden bucket back Visible one step.

title: ecoreview 0.1.18
text: Table row sentence tooltip shows match status (issue #23): hovering cell all_supporting_source_sentences column now renders HTML tooltip identical OCR pane tooltip — matched sentences listed normally unmatched sentences marked ⚠️ italic/muted style. Uses ecrUnmatchedByRow JS global (already set setEvidenceIndex) keyed DataTables data-row index. column tooltips unchanged.

title: ecoreview 0.1.18
text: dtUpdateCell “Non-table node initialisation” warning: JS handler calling $('#interactiveTable').DataTable() outer Shiny output <div>, inner <table>. Changed $('#interactiveTable table').DataTable() existing API instance returned rather DataTables attempting re-initialisation div.

title: ecoreview 0.1.17
text: Infinite scroll (issue #21): replaced DataTables pagination single scrollable container. rows current document visible without page navigation (paging = FALSE, pagination controls removed dom). existing scrollY container provides bounded scroll area.

title: ecoreview 0.1.16
text: OS-style multi-row selection (issue #19): switched DataTables Select extension style = 'os'. Plain click selects single row; Ctrl/Cmd+click adds removes rows selection; Shift+click selects range. Multi-row delete shows confirmation modal soft-deleting selected rows. Cell edits preserve scroll & sort (issue #20, #22): cell edits now push targeted dtUpdateCell JS message (api.cell().data().draw(false)) instead triggering full table re-render. Scroll position, sort order, page preserved every edit. OCR pane longer scrolls double-click--edit: last_scrolled_row variable tracks row last scrolled . Re-clicking double-clicking row trigger another scrollIntoView.

title: ecoreview 0.1.16
text: replaceData replaced client-side updates: table now renders server = FALSE (required Select extension). DT::replaceData calls replaced table_trigger() row-count changes (delete/add) dtUpdateCell -place cell edits.

title: ecoreview 0.1.15
text: Verified document indicator: document names dropdown prefixed ✓ reviewed_at set, making easy spot already-verified files filter . Copyable filename: small selectable text line dropdown always shows current document’s filename can triple-clicked copied without opening dropdown.

title: ecoreview 0.1.15
text: Uncheck “Show unreviewed” now shows docs: dropdown observer observe() whose reactive dependency input$show_unreviewed_only indirect (via filtered_documents()). certain execution paths transitive link established, unchecking effect. Replaced observeEvent(list(all_documents(), input$show_unreviewed_only), ...) checkbox explicit trigger. handler auto-isolated, also eliminates values$document_id feedback loop document navigation.

title: ecoreview 0.1.14
text: OCR pane scroll preserved cell edit (issue #20): row-selection observer observe() depended values$extracted_df. Every cell edit mutated values$extracted_df, re-triggering observer selected row causing scrollIntoView jump OCR pane back first highlighted sentence. Fixed switching observeEvent(input$interactiveTable_rows_selected) isolating extracted_df reads observer fires actual row-selection changes. JS handler also now respects scroll: false flag -row re-highlight path (e.g. delete) can suppress scroll. PDF scroll preserved add/delete/toggle (issue #20): output$pdfViewer depended all_documents() turn depended values$edit_trigger. Incrementing edit_trigger add/delete/show_deleted caused full iframe re-render, resetting PDF page 1. Fixed reading all_documents() via shiny::isolate() inside pdfViewer re-renders selected document changes. Table sort order preserved add/delete/show_deleted (issue #22): handlers called table_trigger(), destroying recreating entire DataTable instance (therefore sort state). Replaced refresh_table_proxy() helper calls DT::replaceData(), -place update already used cell edits. Sort column, sort direction, scroll position, current page preserved.

title: ecoreview 0.1.14
text: OCR highlighting updates sentences edit: editing all_supporting_source_sentences cell now rebuilds evidence span index resends setEvidenceIndex client. column edits leave OCR HTML completely untouched.

title: ecoreview 0.1.13
text: build_evidence_index: fixed ev_id corruption sentence_to_id set injection-success check. failed injection rolled back counter left mapping pointing next successfully-injected sentence’s span, causing unrelated text highlighted row. build_evidence_index: strip ///<> working HTML matching/injection bold OCR headings like **Table 1** Detection... (rendered Table 1 Detection...) longer block fixed-string span injection. html_to_plain_text: replace / tags spaces stripping adjacent table cells concatenated. Previously, evidence sentences drawn table rows (e.g. infection | 20 | 2 0.391 ± 0.32 ...) never match infection20 stripped infection20 separator tokens. find_best_match_in_html: rewrote token-matching insert [^-Za-z0-9]* every pair adjacent characters within token OCR line-break hyphens (amphis-tome) correctly match un-hyphenated evidence word (amphistome).

title: ecoreview 0.1.12
text: OCR viewer now renders page footers table footnotes. Content page$(e.g. page_footer, abbreviation keys) rendered table annotates. Items type title skipped avoid duplication.

title: ecoreview 0.1.11
text: Fixed regression introduced 0.1.10 stripping whitespace HTML plain-text extraction inadvertently broke fixed-string span injection, causing nothing highlighted.

title: ecoreview 0.1.10
text: Evidence sentences split across OCR line breaks now match. working HTML whitespace-normalised (\\s+ → single space) plain-text extraction sentence broken across lines raw OCR still aligns flat plain text used matching.

title: ecoreview 0.1.9
text: OCR tooltip redesigned: two-column layout four sentences present, vertically centred cursor, click--expand scrollable modal showing supporting sentences full.

title: ecoreview 0.1.9
text: Tooltip sentence modal non-functional: IIFE created modal element via document.body.appendChild() <body> existed (script runs <head>), aborting entire IIFE silently dropping event listeners. Fixed lazy initialisation (getSentModal()) mirroring existing getTooltip() pattern.

title: ecoreview 0.1.8
text: Evidence matching simplified single punctuation-normalised, case-insensitive regex pass. Replaces previous three-tier approach (exact → word-regex → sliding-window cosine). non-alphanumeric runs evidence treated equivalent non-alphanumeric separator OCR text, “Table 1.” matches “TABLE 1:” without fuzzy scoring therefore false positives. Markdown tables Mistral OCR now render proper HTML tables. commonmark called extensions = TRUE enable GFM pipe-table support. LaTeX-style superscripts used Mistral OCR (^{}, ^{b}, …) converted <sup> tags rendering.

title: ecoreview 0.1.8
text: Evidence span highlighting completely broken: row_map sending bare integers [0, 1, 2] JS handler expected [{id, tier}] objects, every querySelectorAll('[data-ev-id="undefined"]') found nothing. Tooltip longer shows ✓ checkmark matched sentences; warning ⚠️ kept sentences found OCR text.

title: ecoreview 0.1.7
text: OCR viewer table longer block document load. build_evidence_index running O(n×m) cosine-trigram sliding window inside high-priority Shiny observer, preventing renderDataTable executing. Evidence span injection now deferred via session$onFlushed() OCR table appear immediately; spans injected following flush. table_trigger now incremented outside records tryCatch table always re-renders switching documents, even record loading fails. render_tensorlake_html now supports Mistral OCR format ({"index": N, "markdown": "..."} per page) addition tensorlake format. Previously, documents OCR’d Mistral showed blank preview.

title: ecoreview 0.1.6
text: OCR highlighting rewritten: evidence spans now pre-injected HTML per document load (build_evidence_index) rather re-rendered every row selection. Row switching pure client-side CSS toggle server round-trip, eliminating OCR panel flash. find_best_match_in_html gains exact_only parameter; build_evidence_index uses exact substring matching (instant) get_highlight_matches retains fuzzy sliding-window path callers. html_to_plain_text helper added clean entity-decoded plain text extraction used span injection pipeline. third-party JS/CSS assets vendored locally (inst/app/www/): split.js 1.6.5 mark.js 8.11.1. Font Awesome CDN replaced fontawesome R package. Eliminates runtime CDN dependencies. fontawesome added Imports.

title: ecoreview 0.1.6
text: Removed polyfill.io script tag (CDN compromised 2024 injected malicious JavaScript). Removed MathJax CDN dependency (unused OCR rendering refactor).

title: ecoreview 0.1.5
text: split.js CDN URL pinned v1.6.5 (unpinned, supply-chain risk).

title: ecoreview 0.1.4
text: Removed MathJax CDN script config block. Removed polyfill.io script (compromised CDN, initial removal).

title: ecoreview 0.1.3
text: Package version number displayed next GitHub link title bar. Hover tooltip OCR highlighted sentences shows full list supporting sentences selected row.

title: ecoreview 0.1.3
text: Fixed unescaped double quotes tooltip JS string caused app.R parse errors.

title: ecoreview 0.1.2
text: See git log earlier history.
